user_query:
I'm a local tour guide in Newcastle and I want a small, reproducible data-capture tool I can run on my laptop to assemble an offline snapshot of official visitor information I often reference on tours. Please set up a minimal project environment and a runnable job that does all of the following using the provided input/input_queries.yaml:

1) Environment and run setup
- Provide either a Python virtual environment setup (requirements.txt and a run script) or a Dockerfile (preferred) that builds a runnable image. Name the image newcastle-snapshot if you use Docker.
- The tool must accept input/input_queries.yaml as its only input and produce all artifacts under output/ and logs/.

2) Web search and selection (Internet required)
- For each query in input/input_queries.yaml, perform a live web search using a general search engine and log the query text, timestamp, search engine used, top_n considered (at least 5), and the final chosen URL(s).
- From each query, select at least one relevant page from the specified official domain pattern for that query (e.g., newcastle.gov.uk for City Council content, nexus.org.uk for Tyne and Wear Metro, visitbritain.com for national tourism). Do not hardcode URLs. Record your choices in logs/search_log.jsonl.

3) Download and archive source pages (Internet required)
- Download the full HTML of each selected page and save it under output/raw_html/<domain>/<slug>.html. The filename can be derived from the URL path. Ensure each saved file maps unambiguously back to its URL.
- Create output/manifest.json summarizing: queries_total, pages_downloaded, pages_per_domain (object), html_files (list of relative paths), and items_records (see below). Use only local information to compute these counts.

4) Extract structured content (Internet required for the initial download; extraction must run locally on the downloaded files)
- From each downloaded HTML page, extract these fields: source_domain, source_url, page_title, h1 (first H1 text if present), emails (array), phones (array, UK formats if found), pdf_links (array of absolute URLs to any linked PDFs), retrieved_at (ISO 8601 timestamp for when you downloaded).
- Write one JSON object per line to output/extracted/items.jsonl with the above fields. If a field isn’t present on a page, use an empty array or null where appropriate. All records must be derived only from the saved HTML files in output/raw_html/.

5) Cross-validation and reproducibility
- Ensure that for every record in output/extracted/items.jsonl there is a corresponding HTML file in output/raw_html/ and a corresponding entry in logs/search_log.jsonl. The counts in output/manifest.json must match the number of saved HTML files and extracted records.
- Include a brief README in the project root with run instructions for both: (a) running locally with Python (using requirements.txt) and (b) running in Docker, including the exact command(s) to produce the outputs from input/input_queries.yaml.

Required inputs and outputs:
- Input (provided): input/input_queries.yaml
- Outputs (must be created by your tool):
  - logs/search_log.jsonl (one line per query with search details and chosen URLs)
  - output/raw_html/<domain>/<slug>.html (one file per selected page)
  - output/extracted/items.jsonl (one JSON object per selected page with required fields)
  - output/manifest.json (aggregate counts and file list)
  - README.md (run instructions; list of dependencies)
  - If you use Docker: Dockerfile that builds a working image and runs the job end-to-end

Constraints and notes:
- Do not include or rely on any hardcoded URLs; all target pages must be discovered via live search at runtime and must belong to the official domains indicated in the input file.
- The job must succeed even if page layouts differ; extraction should be robust to basic variation. Do not assume a specific page structure beyond standard HTML tags.
- Only the local artifacts listed above will be used to verify success (presence, structure, internal consistency of counts and mappings). All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
